growing_steps.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
growing_steps.details: Use high-germination, plump seeds. Soak seeds in a solution of fungicides (e.g., Carbendazim) for 24 hours to prevent seed-borne diseases.

growing_steps.title: Nursery Preparation
growing_steps.details: Sow the treated seeds in a well-prepared nursery bed. Maintain adequate moisture and apply light nitrogen if seedlings appear pale.

growing_steps.title: Land Preparation (Puddling)
growing_steps.details: Plough the main field and flood it. Puddle the soil to create a slurry of mud and water, which reduces water percolation and weed growth.

growing_steps.title: Transplanting
growing_steps.details: Transplant 20-25 days old seedlings into the puddled field. Maintain a spacing of 20x10 cm or 20x15 cm for optimal plant density.

growing_steps.title: Water Management
growing_steps.details: Keep the field flooded with 2-5 cm of water during the early vegetative stage. Implement Alternate Wetting and Drying (AWD) to save water without yield loss.

growing_steps.title: Weed & Pest Management
growing_steps.details: Perform periodic manual weeding or use mechanical cono-weeders. Monitor for stem borers and blast disease; apply recommended IPM practices.

growing_steps.title: Harvesting
growing_steps.details: Harvest when 80% of the panicles turn straw-colored and the grain moisture drops to around 20%. Thresh and dry grains to 14% moisture for storage.

growing_steps.title: Seed Selection & Treatment
growing_steps.details: Select high-yielding hybrid varieties suitable for your region. Treat seeds with fungicides (like Thiram or Captan) to protect against soil-borne pathogens.

growing_steps.title: Land Preparation
growing_steps.details: Plough the field deeply using a mouldboard plough, followed by 2-3 harrowings. Incorporate well-decomposed Farm Yard Manure (FYM) during the final ploughing.

growing_steps.title: Sowing
growing_steps.details: Sow the seeds in ridges or flat beds with a spacing of 60x20 cm. Sowing depth should be 3-5 cm to ensure proper germination and root establishment.

growing_steps.title: Water Management
growing_steps.details: Irrigate immediately after sowing if soil moisture is low. Critical stages for irrigation are silking and grain-filling; ensure no water stress during these periods.

growing_steps.title: Weed Management
growing_steps.details: Apply pre-emergence herbicides like Atrazine to control early weed growth. Perform manual weeding at 30 days and earth up the plants to prevent lodging.

growing_steps.title: Pest & Disease Management
growing_steps.details: Monitor for Fall Armyworm and stem borers. Apply neem-based pesticides or recommended chemical sprays (e.g., Emamectin benzoate) at the threshold level.

growing_steps.title: Harvesting
growing_steps.details: Harvest when the cobs turn yellow and the grains harden (black layer formation at the base). Dry the harvested cobs to 12-14% moisture before shelling.

growing_steps.title: Nursery Preparation & Sowing
growing_steps.details: Prepare raised nursery beds. Sow high-quality seeds in lines and cover with a thin layer of soil and mulch. Irrigate daily with a rose can.

growing_steps.title: Land Preparation
growing_steps.details: Plough the main field 4-5 times to achieve a fine tilth. Incorporate 10-15 tons of well-rotted Farm Yard Manure (FYM) per acre.

growing_steps.title: Transplanting
growing_steps.details: Transplant 25-30 days old healthy seedlings in the late afternoon. Maintain a spacing of 60x45 cm. Provide light irrigation immediately.

growing_steps.title: Staking and Pruning
growing_steps.details: Support indeterminate varieties with bamboo stakes or strings to prevent fruit contact with the soil. Prune side shoots to improve air circulation and fruit size.

growing_steps.title: Water Management
growing_steps.details: Drip irrigation is highly recommended to maintain uniform soil moisture. Avoid prolonged dry spells followed by heavy watering, which causes fruit cracking.

growing_steps.title: Pest & Disease Management
growing_steps.details: Monitor for fruit borers, whiteflies, and early blight. Use yellow sticky traps and apply appropriate bio-pesticides or fungicides preventively.

growing_steps.title: Harvesting
growing_steps.details: Harvest fruits at the correct maturity stage (e.g., breaker stage for long-distance transport, fully ripe for local markets) by gently twisting them off the vine.
